title: viola hirta subsp. sepincola
intro: Viola hirta subsp. sepincola, also known as the hairy violet, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the viola genus. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Viola hirta subsp. sepincola include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation and soothe digestive issues.
